data.body:
=== DESIGN DOC: our-review-dimensions.md ===

# Our rebar plan-review gate — current dimension set (design state)

CONTEXT: rebar gate fires at open→in_progress. `claim` = FAST check for a valid signed plan-review
attestation; heavy review runs OUT-OF-BAND. This is a REVIEW PROCESS (returns verdict + findings) —
**NOT an interactive dialog**. The CLIENT (calling agent) revises the plan and re-runs the review to get a
fresh signature; rebar does not own a revise loop (mirrors completion-verifier: returns FAIL+findings,
blocks close, client fixes + recloses). Routing pivots on `has_children`.

VERDICT MODEL: per-dimension PASS / AMBIGUOUS / FAIL. AMBIGUOUS = non-blocking, escalates to higher-tier
adjudicator. FAIL→block is per-dimension TBD (by severity / dimension / measured false-positive rate;
judgment dims MAY block, not only deterministic). Blue-team/adjudicator filter suppresses FP findings.
Abstain trigger = calibrated multi-sample agreement, not raw confidence.

EXEC MODELS: DET (deterministic, no LLM) · 1-TURN (single cheap LLM eval) · 2-STEP (two decoupled LLM calls) ·
AGENT (multi-step tool-using). Cost ladder: DET prefilter → cheap → AGENT on residual.
DETERMINISTIC-TOOL PRINCIPLE: all language-specific deterministic checks FAIL OPEN on unsupported
languages/ecosystems (e.g. Terraform) — never hallucinate, never fail-closed; skip + note.

================================================================
## TIER 0 — DETERMINISTIC PREFILTER (DET, fail-open on unsupported langs)
================================================================
- P1 Readiness shape: has_children XOR has_plan; `## Acceptance Criteria` present (STANDARDIZED across all types). [scenario routing]
- P2 File/symbol reference resolution: named files/symbols/imports in plan resolve in repo via **LSP/tree-sitter** (NOT llm-grep — that's the FP source). [sc.3]
- P3 Package/dependency existence: declared new imports exist in manifest+lockfile (+optional registry). [sc.3 / U1 slopsquat]
- P4 Oversize signals: AC-count, file_impact-count, conjunction/CRUD-verb scan, layers/interfaces touched; **config-tunable per project**; absorbs plan-length-too-long as a minor sub-signal. [sc.1]
- P5 Task-DAG validity: acyclic, explicit ordering, no shared-file write collision (multi-step leaf). [—]
- P7 Destructive/irreversible sniff: ESCALATION SIGNAL ONLY (→T4); never pass/fail. [U4]

================================================================
## TIER 1 — JUDGMENT, ONE-TURN / TWO-STEP (cheap, abstain-capable)
================================================================
- F1 Criteria measurability & **agent-in-session-completability**: each AC is observable pass/fail, NOT subjective/effort; external ops (deploy/migrate/check-logs/run-CLI) ALLOWED if an agent can complete them in-session with provided auth; REJECT ACs requiring real-user feedback / long waits / host-project live execution. [DSO success_measurable + session-verifiability/end-state-litmus; antipattern: do NOT demand production/real-user validation]
- F4 User/problem present (light content check): ticket names the user/stakeholder + problem being solved. [DSO user_impact, understanding-gate content — verify ticket CARRIES it, not a dialog. validation_signal folds into F1's agent-completability]
- E1 Criteria↔description coherence (bidirectional; no silent drop/contradiction). [sc.7]
- E2 Ambiguity / executable-without-clarification (undefined terms, TBD/placeholder) + **spike/research/investigate steps = undercooked-plan signal → clarify**. [sc.2]
- E3 Intent fidelity — **2-STEP**: (1) blind-restate goal from plan ALONE (goal withheld); (2) compare restatement vs stated goal/parent intent; child judged vs its SLICE of parent, not whole. [failure-mode #1; CoVe factored]
- E5 Testing-plan completeness: reviewer has no tests, but TDD ⇒ leaf plan must carry enough detail to assess **behavioral + contractual** coverage & change-detector avoidance; insufficient detail ⇒ flags risk of narrow/tightly-coupled tests. [sc.5; DSO behavioral-testing-standard]
- E6 Verification/termination completeness: each risky step has a following verify/checkpoint; explicit stop condition. Absorbs self-review-as-verification flag. [U5/U6]
- G5 Decomposition judgment: too-big-to-one-shot / should-have-children even if childless; consumes P4 signals. [sc.1; distinct from P4 = signal vs judgment]

================================================================
## TIER 2 — JUDGMENT, AGENT (tool-grounded, primed by deterministic tools, fail-open)
================================================================
- E4 Premise soundness / anti-sycophancy: plan builds on a ticket premise the codebase CONTRADICTS (needs codebase read) + **blue-team filter** turn to suppress over-critical FPs. [U8]
- G1+G2 Edit-set / scope accuracy (MERGED, ORDERED): (1) evaluate the **edit target** first — handles modify-existing (verify locus) vs create-new (verify placement plausibility, no existing target); (2) THEN run deterministic consumer/reference lookup on that target. Checks plan's impact set = real set: no hallucinated, no missing consumers. High blast radius is NOT a fail. Fail-open on unsupported langs. [sc.3+4 / U2+U7]
- G3 Child coverage: children fully+accurately encompass parent criteria (3-condition standard). [sc.8]
- G4 Child consistency: shared-state/conflicting-assumptions/ordering/overlap/residual-refs among children. [sc.8]

================================================================
## TIER 3 — TRIGGERED OVERLAYS (selected by a ROUTER-LLM turn, not naive keywords)
================================================================
Router LLM reads plan + DET signals → decides which overlays are RELEVANT (should this plan consider X?),
bias toward recall. Each overlay then judges ADEQUACY (relevant AND addressed well, not superficially).
- T1 Prior-art / novel-architecture justification (alternatives + limitation + advantage + survey). [sc.6; AGENT web/GitHub]
- T2 Empirical probe (red→green artifact / spike for risky moving parts). [probe idea; AGENT]
- T3 Integration feasibility (external API/dep vs docs+working examples; CLI --help). [AGENT]
- T4 Compat/destructiveness as EXPLICIT JUSTIFIED CHOICE — bidirectional: flag BOTH unacknowledged breakage AND **gratuitous backward-compat / unnecessary feature-flags / migration scaffolding** (YAGNI debt). [P7→here]
- T5a/b/c/d Performance / Reliability / Security / Accessibility — separately triggered; 1-TURN default, AGENT where codebase-grounding needed.
- T6 UX non-happy-path (validation errors, timeouts, empty/partial, entry-exit). [1-TURN]
- T7 Documentation: new docs needed + **existing docs the change invalidates** + NOT excessive / non-navigable / **bloating hot-path agent instructions (CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md)**. [DSO freshness + LLM-doc antipattern]
- T8 LLM/prompt-plan antipatterns (bot-psychologist 17-pt taxonomy). Trigger: plan touches LLM-facing instructions/prompts/agents/hooks.

## OPEN / FLAGGED
- F2 no_overlap (cross-ticket scope duplication) + F3 dependency_aware (prereq tickets identified): PERF/ACCURACY CONCERN — backlog scan is imprecise/context-heavy. Options: (a) reuse rebar's deterministic file_impact-overlap (next_batch already does conflict-aware batching) + explicit depends_on/blocks link presence; (b) DEFER semantic cross-ticket duplication to a follow-on effort. LEAN: deterministic-only now, semantic later.
- E7 self-review-as-verification: folded into E6. E8 test-gaming: DROPPED (post-impl, → code-review/close gate).
- Understanding gate = REVIEW CONTENT criteria (who/problem present, no unresolved assumptions), NOT interactive dialog.
